lesson.menu_desc: Share and Defend Your View with I Think & In My View -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Expressing Opinions -- Opinion Phrases for Debates
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 7 -- Expressing Opinions

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I have been thinking about the client's proposal, and I want to share my point of view before our discussion. In my view, the new timeline is too tight. I think we should ask for two more weeks. My main argument is simple: quality matters more than speed on a contract this size. I know the client wants it fast, so we will need to persuade them. I believe we can find common ground if we offer a clear plan. I do not want to convince them with pressure, only with good reasons. Please think about your own stance, and we can compare our views tomorrow. Talk soon.

Hi team, this is Simone with a quick recap of our discussion about the client's proposal. Daniel and I shared our points of view openly. At first we did not agree on the timeline. In my view, the deadline was too tight, and Daniel agreed. Our main argument was that quality matters most on a large contract. We decided to persuade the client with a clear plan instead of pressure. After a short debate, we found common ground: we will ask for ten extra days, not two weeks. I want to emphasize one point. We should present one united stance to the client. If you disagree, please tell me before Friday. Thanks for your great work, and let us move forward together.

extra_audio.text: Daniel shares his point of view in a voice note before the discussion. Simone reads the discussion brief and prepares her own stance. They meet and compare their opinions about the timeline. After a short debate, they find common ground on a new plan. Simone writes a recap and sends one united stance to the client.
